title: Anonymous Functions and Functions as Objects
description: Functions can be created on the fly and passed around like data.

slides.title: Functions are Objects
slides.content: In R, functions are first-class objects. You can assign them to variables, pass them as arguments, and return them from other functions.

slides.title: Function as Argument
Anonymous Functions
slides.content:
Sometimes you need a quick, one-off function without naming it. Use `function(...) { ... }` directly.

Common with `lapply`, `sapply`:

```r
lapply(list(1:3, 4:6), function(x) x[1])
```

slides.title: Anonymous Function Example
slides.codeSnippet:
numbers <- list(c(1,2), c(3,4,5), c(6))
# Get first element of each list component
first_elements <- lapply(numbers, function(x) x[1])
print(first_elements)

quizData.question: What does `sapply(1:3, function(x) x^2)` return?
quizData.explanation: The anonymous function squares each element, returning 1,4,9.
